title: Ears Get the Royal Treatment Today!
story: Your ears deserve a celebration, and March 13 is their special day! National Earmuff Day honors those fuzzy, warm ear-huggers that keep us cozy in winter. title: A Baseball Legend's Grand Slam Birthday
story: Batter up! John 'Home Run' Baker stepped into the world in 1886. Born on March 13 in Trappe, Maryland, this future baseball star would earn his nickname by leading the American League in home runs for four straight years. Baker's powerful swing helped the Philadelphia A's clinch three World Series titles, cementing his place in the Baseball Hall of Fame.
activity_question: What nickname did John Baker earn in baseball?
bonus_fact: Baker also led the American League in RBIs (runs batted in) twice during his career.

title: Visionary Designer Born in the Big Apple
story: Jacque Fresco burst onto the scene in Manhattan, New York, on March 13, 1916. This future industrial designer would go on to dream up big ideas for a better world. Fresco became famous for founding the Venus Project, which imagined a society built on new technology and sustainable living.
activity_question: What famous project did Jacque Fresco found?
follow_up_question: What kind of ideas did the Venus Project focus on?

title: A Lens on Our World
story: Yann Arthus-Bertrand first opened his eyes to the world on March 13, 1946. Born in Paris, France, he would grow up to become a renowned director and passionate environmentalist. His camera lens became a powerful tool, capturing Earth's beauty from above and inspiring people to protect our planet.
activity_question: What profession did Yann Arthus-Bertrand become known for?
follow_up_question: How do you think aerial photography can help raise awareness about environmental issues?

title: Drumming Up Change: Julian Bahula's Story
story: Julian Bahula's beats echoed far beyond the stage. Born in Pretoria, South Africa on March 13, 1938, he became a powerful jazz drummer and composer. Bahula used his music to fight against apartheid, a system of racial segregation. He led the band Jabula and moved to Britain, where his rhythms continued to inspire and spread awareness.
activity_question: What type of music was Julian Bahula known for?
follow_up_question: How did Julian Bahula use his music to make a difference?

title: Erma Franklin: A Voice of Soul
story: Erma Franklin, a powerful voice in gospel and R&B music, entered the world on March 13, 1938. Born in Shelby, Mississippi, she grew up to become a talented singer. Erma's soulful rendition of 'Piece Of My Heart' would later inspire many artists. Though she passed away in 2002, her musical legacy lives on.
activity_question: What type of music was Erma Franklin known for?
bonus_fact: Erma was the older sister of the legendary 'Queen of Soul', Aretha Franklin.

title: A Football Hero's Birth
story: Touchdown! A future football star entered the world on March 13, 1938. Joe Bellino, born in Winchester, Massachusetts, would grow up to become a legendary halfback. His amazing skills on the field led him to win the prestigious Heisman Trophy in 1960 while playing for Navy. Bellino's talent and dedication earned him a place in the American College Football Hall of Fame.
activity_question: What prestigious award did Joe Bellino win in 1960?
follow_up_question: Which college team did Joe Bellino play for when he won the Heisman Trophy?

title: Gospel Star's Soulful Journey Begins
story: A powerful voice was born in Hanceville, Alabama in 1940. Canzetta Staton, better known as Candi, would grow up to become a gospel sensation. Her soulful singing and heartfelt lyrics touched many lives. On March 13, the world welcomed the future star who'd later inspire listeners with hits like 'Young Hearts Run Free'.
activity_question: What was Candi Staton's birth name?
bonus_fact: Gospel music often tells stories of hope and faith through song.

title: Rainbow's Colorful Host Born
story: Geoffrey Hayes, the beloved host of the children's TV show Rainbow, entered the world in Stockport, England. Born in 1942 on March 13, Hayes would grow up to become a familiar face on British television. He not only presented Rainbow but also acted in the police drama Z-Cars, bringing joy and entertainment to viewers young and old.
activity_question: What children's TV show did Geoffrey Hayes host?
bonus_fact: Rainbow aired for over 20 years, teaching kids about friendship and problem-solving through colorful puppet characters.

title: Rock 'n' Roll Time Machine: Sha Na Na
story: Boise, Idaho rocked in 1949 when Donny York was born. He grew up to become a cool cat in the band Sha Na Na. These guys brought back the sounds of the 1950s, complete with slicked-back hair and leather jackets. On March 13, York celebrated his birthday, dreaming of the day he'd hit the stage and make people dance!
activity_question: What band did Donny York perform with?
bonus_fact: Sha Na Na performed at the famous Woodstock music festival in 1969!

title: Funny Lady Takes the Stage
story: Laughter echoed through Toronto as Robin Duke entered the world. Born on March 13, 1954, this Canadian comedienne would grow up to tickle funny bones across North America. Duke became famous for her hilarious characters on popular TV shows like Saturday Night Live and SCTV. She even starred in the movie Club Paradise, bringing her unique humor to the big screen.
activity_question: What TV shows did Robin Duke become famous for?
activity_choices: The Office and Parks and Recreation
Whose Line Is It Anyway and The Tonight Show
follow_up_question: What other talents might a comedienne like Robin Duke have?

title: Musical Maestro Born in Argentina
story: A musical star was born in Rosario, Argentina in 1963. Fito Paez, who would grow up to become a famous singer-songwriter and pianist, entered the world on March 13. His talent for creating beautiful melodies and heartfelt lyrics would later shine in albums like La Conquista Del Espacio, captivating audiences across Latin America and beyond.
activity_question: What instrument does Fito Paez play besides being a singer-songwriter?
follow_up_question: Can you name another famous musician from Argentina?

title: Musical Maestro Makes Her Mark
story: Susanna Mälkki, a talented Finnish cellist and conductor, was born in Helsinki on March 13, 1969. Her musical journey led her to lead prestigious groups like the Ensemble InterContemporain and conduct at the famous La Scala opera house. Mälkki's skill with the baton also earned her the role of conductor for the Helsinki Philharmonic Orchestra.
activity_question: What instrument did Susanna Mälkki play besides being a conductor?
follow_up_question: Can you name another famous orchestra besides the Helsinki Philharmonic?
